---
title: "0.1.6 Verification checklist"
author: "Minh-Quan Ton-Ngoc"
date: "2026-05-11"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    toc-expand: true
    number-sections: true
    code-fold: false
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
engine: knitr
---
```{=html}
<style>
.setup-note {
  background: #f8f9fa;
  border-left: 4px solid #0d6efd;
  padding: 1rem 1.2rem;
  border-radius: 0.4rem;
  margin-bottom: 1rem;
}
.check-card {
  background: #f6fff8;
  border-left: 4px solid #198754;
  padding: 1rem 1.2rem;
  border-radius: 0.4rem;
  margin: 1rem 0;
}
.manual-note {
  background: #fff8e1;
  border-left: 4px solid #f0ad4e;
  padding: 0.9rem 1rem;
  border-radius: 0.4rem;
  margin: 1rem 0;
}
.small-text {
  font-size: 0.95rem;
  color: #495057;
}
figure figcaption,
.figure-caption,
p.caption {
  text-align: center;
}
summary {
  font-weight: 600;
}
</style>
```

# Verification checklist

Run the checks for your operating system. It is fine if `java -version` fails when you have not installed Java.

## Windows PowerShell

```powershell
python --version
pip --version
java -version
ssh -V
```

## R console

```r
R.version.string
sapply(c("dplyr", "ggplot2", "phyloseq", "vegan"), requireNamespace, quietly = TRUE)
```

## macOS Terminal

```bash
python3 --version
R --version
java -version
ssh -V
```

## Ubuntu Terminal

Use this on your own Ubuntu computer, VM, or WSL setup:

```bash
python3 --version
python3 -m pip --version
R --version
java -version
ssh -V
```

If you installed optional graphical tools locally, also check:

```bash
blastn -version
makeblastdb -version
seaview
```

<div class="check-card">
If Python, R, SSH, and the R package check work, your laptop is ready for the core workshop activities.
</div>
